englishDefinitions: A chronometer, an instrument that measures time, particularly the time of day.
(attributive) A common noun relating to an instrument that measures or keeps track of time.
(British) The odometer of a motor vehicle.
(electronics) An electrical signal that synchronizes timing among digital circuits of semiconductor chips or modules.
The seed head of a dandelion.
(computing, informal) A CPU clock cycle, or T-state.
(uncountable) A luck-based patience or solitaire card game with the cards laid out to represent the face of a clock.
persianTranslation: کرنومتر، ابزاری که زمان، به ویژه زمان روز را اندازه گیری می کند.
(صفت) اسم مشترک مربوط به ابزاری است که زمان را اندازه می گیرد یا ردیابی می کند.
(الکترونیک) یک سیگنال الکتریکی که زمان بندی را بین مدارهای دیجیتال تراشه ها یا ماژول های نیمه هادی هماهنگ می کند.
(محاسبات، غیر رسمی) سیکل ساعت CPU یا حالت T.
(غیرقابل شمارش) یک بازی کارتی مبتنی بر شانس یا بازی یک نفره با کارت هایی که برای نمایش چهره یک ساعت چیده شده اند.
examples: A 12-hour clock system; an antique clock sale; Acme is a clock manufacturer.
This car has over 300,000 miles on the clock.
I can't go off to lunch yet: I'm still on the clock.
We let the guys use the shop's tools and equipment for their own projects as long as they're off the clock.

Arthur Morrison, Chance of the Game
But if the clock was a red 'un, and the opportunity undoubted; to be pinched in the Bow Road merely might well imply loss of caste in the mob, but nobody need be ashamed to be pinched anywhere for a gold watch, after all.
